panels.title: Produce Rate
panels.description: Shows the current aggregate message produce rate across all Kafka topics in messages per second. A sudden drop may indicate producer failures or broker unavailability.

panels.title: Consume Rate
panels.description: Shows the current aggregate message consume rate across all consumer groups in messages per second. Compare with produce rate to detect growing lag.

panels.title: Max Consumer Lag
panels.description: Shows the highest consumer lag across all consumer groups and partitions. A rising value signals that consumers are falling behind producers.

panels.title: Produce p95 Latency
panels.description: Shows the 95th percentile latency for producing messages to Kafka. Watch for spikes that may indicate broker back-pressure or network issues.

panels.title: Message Produce Rate by Topic (msg/s)
panels.description: Tracks the per-topic message produce throughput over time. Look for uneven distribution or sudden drops that may signal producer issues on specific topics.

panels.title: Publish Latency p95 by Topic (ms)
panels.description: Tracks the 95th percentile publish latency for each topic over time. Spikes on individual topics may indicate partition hotspots or slow broker acknowledgements.

panels.title: Message Consume Rate by Topic (msg/s)
panels.description: Tracks the per-topic message consume throughput over time. Compare with produce rate to identify topics where consumers are not keeping up.

panels.title: Receive Latency p95 by Topic (ms)
panels.description: Tracks the 95th percentile receive latency for each topic over time. Elevated values may point to slow consumer polling or network congestion.

panels.title: Consume Rate by Consumer Group (msg/s)
panels.description: Tracks message consumption throughput for each consumer group over time. Uneven rates across groups may indicate processing bottlenecks in specific consumers.

panels.title: Process Rate by Consumer Group (msg/s)
panels.description: Tracks the rate at which each consumer group processes messages after receiving them. A gap between consume and process rates suggests slow message handling logic.

panels.title: Consumer Lag by Group
panels.description: Tracks the total consumer lag for each consumer group over time. Steadily increasing lag indicates consumers cannot keep pace with incoming messages.

panels.title: Consumer Lag per Partition
panels.description: Shows lag broken down by topic, partition, and consumer group. Use this to identify hot partitions where a single partition drives the majority of lag.

panels.title: Rebalance Rate by Group (rebalances/s)
panels.description: Tracks consumer group rebalance frequency over time. Frequent rebalances cause processing pauses and may indicate unstable consumers or aggressive session timeouts.

panels.title: Failed Heartbeat Rate by Group (failures/s)
panels.description: Tracks the rate of failed consumer heartbeats per group. Rising failures typically precede rebalances and may indicate network issues or overloaded consumers.

panels.title: Assigned Partitions by Group
panels.description: Tracks the number of partitions assigned to each consumer group over time. Sudden changes indicate rebalances; uneven distribution may cause processing skew.

panels.title: Produce Latency p95 by Topic (ms)
panels.description: Tracks end-to-end produce latency at the 95th percentile for each topic. Sustained increases may signal broker disk pressure or replication delays.

panels.title: Receive Latency p95 by Topic (ms)
panels.description: Tracks end-to-end receive latency at the 95th percentile for each topic. Watch for divergence across topics that may reveal consumer-side bottlenecks.

panels.title: Process Latency p95 by Topic (ms)
panels.description: Tracks end-to-end message processing latency at the 95th percentile for each topic. High values point to slow downstream processing logic after message consumption.

panels.title: Produce Errors by Error Type (errors/s)
panels.description: Tracks producer-side error rates grouped by error type over time. Spikes in specific error types help pinpoint issues like serialization failures or authorization errors.

panels.title: Consume Errors by Error Type (errors/s)
panels.description: Tracks consumer-side error rates grouped by error type over time. Watch for deserialization or offset commit failures that may cause message reprocessing.

panels.title: Process Errors by Error Type (errors/s)
panels.description: Tracks message processing error rates grouped by error type over time. Persistent errors suggest bugs in consumer business logic or downstream dependency failures.

panels.title: Open Connections to Brokers
panels.description: Tracks the number of active client connections to each Kafka broker over time. A sudden drop may indicate broker unavailability or client connectivity issues.

panels.title: Client Operation Duration p95 by Operation (ms)
panels.description: Tracks the 95th percentile duration for Kafka client operations such as poll, commit, and send. Elevated durations reveal which client operations are contributing to overall latency.
